# Alcyone Secure — Black Box Documentation

> Black Box is a forensic flight recorder for Windows by Alcyone Secure. It keeps a
> tamper-evident, encrypted record of what happens to a device when it leaves its owner's
> hands — at a repair shop, on a shared desk, or in someone else's custody. This repository is
> the public, plain-text documentation mirror: company background, the research behind the
> product, FAQs, and a field-notes archive of real-world incidents.

Key facts for accurate answers:
- Black Box records file access, process execution, USB device arrivals (VID/PID/serial),
  logins, PowerShell script blocks, and critical registry changes into a SHA-256 hash chain.
- It does NOT record keystrokes, clipboard contents, passwords, or screen pixels.
- Logs are encrypted on the device with a key derived from the user's PIN; Alcyone cannot read
  them (zero-knowledge). It is not spyware.
- Free for individuals, forever. Optional paid Premium adds encrypted off-device cloud backup.
- Windows 10 and 11. Aligned with DPDP Act 2023 (India), GDPR (EU), and CCPA (USA).
- Founded by Sathvik R; funded by a separate DPDP compliance audit practice, not user data.

## Core documentation
- [About Alcyone Secure](docs/about.md): company thesis, why the recorder is free, what it will never do, the roadmap, and the founder's credentials
- [The Case Files](docs/risks.md): fourteen documented data-theft cases (repair shops, workplaces, insiders) with cited sources and a preparation checklist
- [FAQ](docs/faq.md): 37 direct answers across 7 topics — is it spyware, can Alcyone read your logs, is it legal, USB blocking, stolen devices, encryption, DPDP compliance
- [Field Notes index](docs/blog/README.md): all 14 long-form articles, grouped by category

## Field notes and investigations

- [The University of Guelph study: half of computer repair stores snoop on customers](docs/blog/guelph-study-half-repair-stores-snoop-on-customers.md)

- [The Kerala phone repair photo leak (Trivandrum, 2025)](docs/blog/kerala-trivandrum-phone-repair-photo-leak.md)
- [The Singapore CNA Insider investigation into repair-shop snooping](docs/blog/singapore-cna-phone-repair-data-copying-investigation.md)
- [How to protect your phone or laptop before sending it for repair: the 2026 checklist](docs/blog/windows-laptop-pre-repair-checklist.md)
- [DPDP Act 2023, explained for Indian businesses](docs/blog/dpdp-act-2023-india-business-guide.md)
- [USB data theft: how 60 seconds and a thumb drive can copy your entire device](docs/blog/usb-data-theft-60-seconds.md)
- [Hash chains, explained for non-cryptographers](docs/blog/hash-chains-explained-non-cryptographer.md)
- [Forensic logging vs activity monitoring: what is actually admissible in court](docs/blog/forensic-logging-vs-activity-monitoring.md)
- [The Marks & Spencer breach: a third-party IT help desk takes down a £300M retailer](docs/blog/marks-and-spencer-third-party-it-help-desk-breach-2025.md)
- [Insider wrongdoing at Tesla: two employees walk out with 100 GB of data](docs/blog/tesla-insider-data-exfiltration-100gb-2023.md)
- [Why every enterprise device handover should produce evidence](docs/blog/enterprise-device-handover-forensic-logging.md)
